key_insight: OmicFormer is a Transformer model that bakes in statistical priors about omics features to improve disease and trait prediction across diverse datasets.
clinical_relevance: By improving robustness under distribution shift, the approach could support more reliable omics-based risk prediction and biomarker discovery in precision medicine settings.
method: Uses a Transformer architecture tailored for high-dimensional omics data.
Integrates two priors: feature-label associations and feature-feature dependencies.
Learns local and long-range biological interactions to boost generalization.
evidence_signal: In 500,000 UK Biobank participants, OmicFormer outperformed strong baselines across 450 disease and 900 trait tasks, and also improved generalization in independent proteomics and multi-site neuroimaging cohorts.
watch_item: Performance gains need careful validation across additional populations and assay types, since omics predictions can still be affected by cohort shift, platform differences, and label quality.
paper_title: OmicFormer: a statistical priors-informed transformer for accurate and generalizable omics prediction of diseases and complex traits
